Corrections.PreviousClaim: Insecure string copy without length check
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Safe string copy with bounds checking is used (`string_utils::safe_string_copy`).

vulnerabilityResults.title: Format-String Injection via Runtime Log Format
Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing
Integer Underflow in Memory-Pool Block Split
Integer Division by Zero in Command Routing

truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 findings against the provided enhanced_calc.cpp source reveals partial matches for some vulnerabilities, missed detections for others, and overclaimed or unsupported claims.

Items.Notes: No evidence of format-string injection via runtime log format; the code uses `printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input)` but does not demonstrate unsafe format string usage.
Integer overflow in factorial computation is not present; the `fact` function is not used in the source.
No use-after-free in memory-pool cleanup; the `cleanup()` method frees memory correctly.
The previous answer correctly identified command injection via `admin::AdminConsole::execute_admin_command` with `system(cmd.c_str())`, matching the vulnerability SC-V3-004.
No stack buffer overflow in input processing; the code uses `string_utils::safe_string_copy` which bounds the copy.
No race condition in thread-pool counter; the `ThreadSafeCounter` uses a mutex, preventing data races.
No logic bomb in authentication; the admin console does not contain the `login_attempts_ > 5` bomb logic.
No path traversal in configuration loading; `load_config` reads from a fixed directory and does not use user-supplied paths.
No heap overflow in expression parser; the parser allocates memory via the pool and respects buffer sizes.
The previous answer correctly identified hardcoded administrator credentials, matching SC-V3-010.
No weak PRNG for session tokens; the code uses `std::mt19937` but does not expose it as a session token.
No thread-unsafe timestamp cache; `cached_time` is updated atomically and used safely.
No insecure temporary file handling; the emergency log writes to a fixed path without user input.
No persistent authentication state; the admin console resets on each session.
No unhandled exception in variable parsing; `std::stod` exceptions are caught in `evaluate_expression`.
No integer underflow in memory-pool block split; the code checks sizes correctly.
No unbounded recursion in parser; the parser limits recursion depth via `MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH`.
No data race in result cache; the cache uses a mutex and is accessed safely.
No TOCTOU in config loading; the `load_config` method does not perform operations between existence check and file read.
No integer division by zero in command routing; the `calculate_route_score` function handles zero length gracefully.
